# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD for Python Application

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@main

    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@main
      with:
        python-version: '3.x'

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install -r requirements.txt

    - name: Run tests
      run: |
        python -m unittest discover -s tests

  deploy:
    needs: build-and-test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@main

    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@main
      with:
        python-version: '3.x'

    - name: Deploy application
      # Replace this step with the actual deployment action or script
      run: echo "Deploying the application..."
